assertion_results.text: The answer recommends writing a failing test first and clearly references the TDD cycle or red-green-refactor order
assertion_results.evidence: Explicitly states: "Recommended TDD workflow (Red → Green → Refactor)" and includes steps: "Write the first test (fail on purpose)" and "Run tests to see it fail (Red)".

assertion_results.text: The answer says to write only the minimal implementation needed to make the test pass before refactoring
assertion_results.evidence: Step 4: "Implement the minimal code to pass the test (Green) - Write the simplest implementation that only satisfies the current test. - Avoid generalizing early." Followed by Step 6: "Refactor (Refactor)".

assertion_results.text: The answer stays focused on workflow order instead of expanding into general tooling setup
assertion_results.evidence: Includes tooling and extras beyond workflow order: commands like "pytest -q", coverage "pytest --cov=mypkg --cov-report=term-missing", property-based testing with "Use Hypothesis", and a section "Handy pytest tips while iterating: - pytest -q -x --lf ... - pytest -k my_helper ...".
